name: Soberton Heath
overview: Soberton Heath is a small rural hamlet in the civil parish of Soberton in the City of Winchester district of Hampshire, in the Meon Valley between Bishop's Waltham and Wickham, a little south of Soberton village and the A32. It is a scattered settlement of houses, bungalows and smallholdings on former heathland, set in farmland on the edge of the South Downs. Soberton Heath has no shops of its own; the nearest pub (the White Lion) and church are in Soberton, with fuller amenities in Bishop's Waltham, Wickham and Droxford nearby.
character: A quiet, dispersed rural hamlet of detached houses and smallholdings in open Meon Valley countryside near the South Downs. The atmosphere is peaceful and semi-rural, with lanes, paddocks and footpaths, and the larger village of Soberton and the Meon Valley Trail close by.
amenities: No shop in Soberton Heath itself
The White Lion Inn and St Peter's Church in Soberton village (~1 mi)
Shops, services and schools in Bishop's Waltham (~3-4 mi) and Wickham (~3 mi)
Droxford village amenities and the Meon Valley Trail nearby
South Downs National Park countryside on the doorstep

schools.name: Newtown Soberton Infant School (Meon Valley Federation)

commutes.notes: Nearest town for shops and services
Via the A32/A272; mainline station to London Waterloo
From Botley or Petersfield (~9-10 mi), or faster from Winchester

prices.summary: Soberton Heath housing is mostly detached houses, bungalows and smallholdings on generous plots; the Meon Valley SO32 market generally runs from the mid-£400ks for smaller homes to £1m+ for larger country properties, with limited turnover.

thingsToDo: Walking and cycling the Meon Valley Trail (former railway)
South Downs National Park footpaths around Soberton and Droxford
Fishing and river walks on the River Meon
Country pubs of the Meon Valley
placesToEat: The White Lion Inn, Soberton (~1 mi)
Pubs in Droxford and Hambledon nearby
Wide choice of pubs and cafes in Bishop's Waltham (~3-4 mi)
pros: Quiet, semi-rural Meon Valley setting near the South Downs
Generous plots and countryside on the doorstep
Village pub and infant school close by; towns within ~3-4 miles
Meon Valley Trail for walking and cycling
cons: No shop in the hamlet; car essential
No mainline railway station within easy reach
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a quiet, plot-rich Meon Valley hamlet near the South Downs with village amenities and good schools a short drive away, who are comfortable being car-dependent.
nearestStation: Botley (~9 miles) or Petersfield (~10 miles); Winchester for fast London services

sources.label: Vision of Britain - Soberton (hamlets incl. Newtown/Southend)
Meon Valley Federation (Newtown Soberton Infant School)
